seoTitle: Throssell vs Tincurrin — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Throssell and Tincurrin. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Throssell and Tincurrin.
heading: Throssell vs Tincurrin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Throssell, Western Australia and Tincurrin, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tincurrin has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Throssell (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Throssell or Tincurrin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Throssell has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Tincurrin scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Throssell scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Tincurrin scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Throssell or Tincurrin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tincurrin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.07% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
